questions: Write a program that asks the user for two numbers and then prints the result of adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing them using the appropriate operators.
Write a program that calculates the area and perimeter of a rectangle given its length and width, using multiplication and addition operators.
Write a program that computes the average of three numbers entered by the user, using addition and division operators.
Write a program that swaps the values of two numeric variables without using a temporary variable, by applying addition and subtraction operators.
Write a program that reads an integer from the user and prints whether it is even or odd by using the modulus operator.
Write a program that reads three integers from the user, calculates their sum, product, and average, and prints all three results.
Write a program that asks for a number and prints "Divisible by 3" if the number is divisible by 3, otherwise prints "Not divisible by 3".
Write a program that reads an integer and prints "Divisible by both 3 and 5" if it is divisible by both, otherwise prints "Not divisible by both".
Write a program that asks the user for their age and prints "Adult" if the age is 18 or older, otherwise prints "Minor".
Write a program that takes a score (0-100) as input and prints "Pass" if the score is 35 or higher, otherwise prints "Fail".
Write a program that reads a character and prints "Vowel" if it is a, e, i, o, or u, otherwise prints "Consonant".
Write a program that reads a numeric score (0‑100) and prints "Poor" if the score is less than 35, "Average" if it is between 35 and 70 inclusive, and "Good" if it is greater than 70.
Write a program that prints the numbers from 1 to 10 using a for loop.
Write a program that prints all even numbers between 1 and 20 using a for loop.
Write a program that calculates and prints the sum of the numbers from 1 to 100 using a for loop.
Write a program that prints the multiplication table of 5 (from 5×1 to 5×10) using a for loop.
Write a program that prints numbers from 1 to 10 using a for loop.
Write a program that prints only even numbers between 1 and 20 using a for loop.
Write a program that asks the user to enter their name and age, then prints a sentence like "My name is <name> and I am <age> years old."
Write a program that prompts the user to input three numbers, one after another, and then prints each number on its own line.
Write a program that reads a user's department name and prints "My department is <department>."
Write a program that asks the user to enter their income and then prints "Scholarship available" if the income is greater than 7000, otherwise prints "Not eligible for scholarship".
Write a program that prompts the user to enter an integer and then prints the integer back to the screen.
Write a program that reads a number from the user and prints whether the number is positive, negative, or zero.
Write a program that reads a number as a string from the user, converts it to an integer, adds 5 to it, and prints the result.
Write a program that asks the user for three numbers, converts each input to an integer, and then prints their sum.
Write a program that reads a user's age as input, converts it to an integer, and prints a message stating the age next year.
Write a program that reads three numbers as strings, converts them to integers, and prints their average as a floating‑point number.
Write a program that reads a temperature in Celsius as a string, converts it to a float, and prints the equivalent temperature in Fahrenheit.
Write a program that reads a binary number represented as a string, converts it to an integer, and prints its decimal value.
Write a program that defines a function which takes two integers and prints their sum.
Write a program that defines a function which receives an integer and prints "Even" if the number is even or "Odd" otherwise.
Write a program that defines a function which receives two integers A and B and prints all numbers from A to B inclusive.
Write a program that defines a function which receives a string and prints the string reversed.
Write a program that declares an integer variable, a float variable, and a string variable, then prints each of them on a separate line.
Write a program that reads two numbers from the user, stores them in variables, and prints their sum, difference, product, and quotient.
Write a program that creates two string variables, concatenates them with a space in between, and prints the resulting sentence.
Write a program that swaps the values of two variables using a temporary third variable and prints the values before and after swapping.
Write a program that assigns the value "123" to a variable, converts it to an integer, and prints both the original string and the converted integer.
Write a program that declares variables for a person's name and age, assigns them values, and prints both values on separate lines.
Write a program that reads 5 integers from the user, stores them in a list, and then prints the list in reverse order.
Write a program that creates a list of the first ten square numbers and prints the list.
Write a program that reads a list of words from the user (comma separated) and prints the longest word.
Write a program that removes duplicate elements from a list of numbers entered by the user and prints the resulting list.
Write a program that merges two lists entered by the user and prints the combined list.
Write a program that creates a list of numbers from 0 to 5, inserts the value 11 at index 0, and then prints the updated list.
Write a program that uses nested for loops to print a rectangle of asterisks with 4 rows and 6 columns.
Write a program that prints a multiplication table for numbers 1 through 5 using nested for loops.
Write a program that generates all possible two‑digit combinations (00 to 99) and prints each combination on a separate line using nested loops.
Write a program that asks the user for a number n and then prints a right‑angled triangle of numbers where each row i contains the numbers 1 to i, using nested for loops.
Write a program that prints the numbers from 1 to 5 using a while loop.
Write a program that prints the first 10 natural numbers in reverse order using a while loop.
Write a program that asks the user for a positive integer and then prints its factorial using a while loop.
Write a program that repeatedly asks the user to enter a number and adds it to a running total until the user enters 0, then prints the total sum.
Write a program that takes a list containing duplicate numbers, converts it to a set to remove duplicates, and prints the resulting set.
Write a program that creates an empty set, adds the numbers 1, 2, and 3 to it, and then prints the set.
Write a program that creates a set of strings, attempts to add a duplicate string, and prints the set to show that duplicates are not stored.
Write a program that removes an element from a set using the discard method and prints the set before and after removal.
Write a program that creates a dictionary with keys "name" and "location" and prints the dictionary.
Write a program that updates the value of an existing key in a dictionary and prints the updated dictionary.
Write a program that attempts to add a duplicate key to a dictionary with a different value and prints the dictionary to demonstrate that the last value overwrites the previous one.
Write a program that removes a key-value pair from a dictionary using the pop method and prints the removed value and the resulting dictionary.
